2606.20544.title: Toward Calibrated Mixture-of-Experts Under Distribution Shift
2606.20544.paper_summary: Calibration aligns a model's predictive uncertainty with the frequencies of its empirical outcomes and is important for understanding and trusting reported probabilities. Recent work shows that enforcing calibration at the level of individual predictors can improve ensemble accuracy and calibration, with mixture-of-experts (MoE) models showing strong empirical improvements in particular; however, the conditions under which calibration helps MoE are not well understood. In this work, we study how MoE models behave under distribution shift, focusing on how routing mechanisms interact with expert-level calibration. We show that expert calibration is sufficient to ensure calibration of the overall model under a broad class of distribution shifts in hard-routed models, but is insufficient for calibrating soft-routed models. To address this, we propose an adversarial reweighting that penalizes calibration errors of the routed aggregate under distribution shift, and we demonstrate that it improves the accuracy-calibration tradeoff both on average and on difficult subsets of the data, across model classes, prediction tasks, and distribution shifts.
2606.20544.paper_summary_zh: 校準將模型的預測不確定性與其實證結果的頻率對齊，對於理解和信任報告的概率至關重要。最近的研究顯示，在單個預測器層面強制校準可以提高集成的準確性和校準，特別是專家混合模型（MoE）顯示出強大的實證改進；然而，校準幫助 MoE 的條件尚不清楚。在這項工作中，我們研究了 MoE 模型在分佈轉移下的行為，重點關注路由機制如何與專家級校準互動。我們表明，專家校準足以確保在硬路由模型下，廣泛類別的分佈轉移下整體模型的校準，但對於軟路由模型來說則不足以進行校準。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種對抗性重加權，對於在分佈轉移下路由聚合的校準錯誤進行懲罰，並且我們證明它在平均值和數據的困難子集上改善了準確性與校準的權衡，涵蓋了模型類別、預測任務和分佈轉移。
2606.20544.author: Gina Wong et.al.
2606.20544.authors: Gina Wong, Drew Prinster, Suchi Saria, Rama Chellappa, Anqi Liu
